steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: The goal is to find the quadratic roots and then identify the values of $h$ and $k$ from the radical form of one solution. Use the quadratic formula on $x^2 - 22x + 116 = 0$: $$x=\frac{22\pm\sqrt{(-22)^2-4(1)(116)}}{2}$$ $$x=\frac{22\pm\sqrt{20}}{2}=11\pm\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{20}$$

steps.title: Match the keyed form
steps.content: One solution is $$x=11-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{20}.$$ Comparing this with $x=h-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{k}$ gives $h=11$ and $k=20$.
